issue_title: Marking mail as spam missing on tablet

reasoning_trace.finding: Searched local source only. Key hits were the message view menu XML, MessageViewFragment.prepareMenu(), and messageview_visible_refile_actions settings.
Confirmed the repository is Thunderbird for Android/K-9 shared code.
Confirmed project development context; no external sources used.
Confirmed legacy UI code is outside the new feature API/internal split concern for this localization task.
The message view overflow contains a refile submenu with four declared items: refile_archive, refile_spam, refile_move, and refile_copy. Therefore the XML itself does not omit Archive or Spam.
The settings entry values include archive, move, copy, and spam for messageview_visible_refile_actions.
Found the only runtime visibility handling for refile_archive/refile_spam in MessageViewFragment.prepareMenu().
MessageViewFragment inflates message_view_option_menu in onCreateMenu() and calls prepareMenu() in onPrepareMenu().
When isMoveCapable is true, the refile submenu is shown and refile_move is forced visible, while refile_archive and refile_spam depend on canMessageBeArchived and canMessageBeMovedToSpam.
The visible message actions settings map archive/spam booleans separately from move/copy.
The general settings screen exposes messageview_visible_refile_actions as a MultiSelectListPreference.
The top-level Archive and Spam menu items are gated by visual settings, but the refile submenu visibility is controlled in MessageViewFragment.prepareMenu().
Defaults for message view archive, move, copy, and spam action visibility are false, so any submenu code tied to those booleans would hide Archive and Spam by default.
The display visual settings manager loads and saves the archive/spam visibility booleans using the defaults from DisplayVisualSettings.
Legacy settings descriptions also default messageViewArchiveActionVisible and messageViewSpamActionVisible to false.

canMessageBeArchived returns false if no archive folder is configured or if the current message is already in the archive folder; canMessageBeMovedToSpam returns false under the analogous spam conditions.
Tablet split view uses MessageViewContainerFragment and still ultimately displays a MessageViewFragment; there is no tablet-specific message view menu implementation.
The split-view container creates MessageViewFragment instances from MessageListItem.messageReference and delegates actions to the active MessageViewFragment.
MessageListItem.messageReference is built from account uuid, folderId, and messageUid.
MessageListItem.folderId comes directly from MessageDetailsAccessor.message.folderId.
The message list loader maps stored message rows into MessageListItem objects; it does not apply a tablet-specific action filter.
Eliminated the XML as the primary fault because Archive and Spam submenu items are declared there.
Eliminated the split-view container as the direct visibility fault because it only hosts MessageViewFragment and delegates menu/action behavior.

The symptom exactly matches runtime hiding of refile_archive and refile_spam while refile_move/refile_copy remain visible. The statement controlling refile_spam is the strongest single fault location for the issue title.

fault_candidates.reasoning: The reported missing Spam entry is controlled directly by this statement. The menu XML declares refile_spam, and tablet split view still uses MessageViewFragment.prepareMenu(); therefore the item can disappear only when this assignment sets it invisible during menu preparation.

The same prepareMenu block directly controls the missing Archive entry. It shares the same failure pattern as Spam: the XML contains the item, but this runtime visibility assignment can hide it while Move and Copy stay visible.

final_answer.causal_chain: The user opens a message, the activity uses MessageViewFragment for both phone and tablet/split-view message display, MessageViewFragment inflates message_view_option_menu, the XML contains refile_spam, onPrepareMenu calls prepareMenu(), prepareMenu leaves refile_move and refile_copy visible but assigns refile_spam visibility from canMessageBeMovedToSpam, and when that predicate evaluates false the Spam row is removed from the Refile submenu.
final_answer.fix_hint: Ensure refile_spam and refile_archive visibility is based only on whether the action is actually possible for the current message/account, not on tablet/action-bar presentation state or unrelated visible-action settings.
